faqs.question: How marble is measured
faqs.answer:
Slab Measurement Guidelines for International Marble

Marble slabs are measured centre to centre in terms of length and breadth. If a slab has a broken corner, the damaged area is measured at a 90-degree angle, and its area is deducted from the total slab area.

The attached graphical representation in the link below illustrates the centre-to-centre measurement method.

https://stonexindia-my.sharepoint.com/my?id=%2Fpersonal%2Fprakhar%5Fgupta%5Fstonexglobal%5Fcom%2FDocuments%2FPictures%2FScreenshots%2FScreenshot%202026%2D02%2D19%20113711%2Epng&parent=%2Fpersonal%2Fprakhar%5Fgupta%5Fstonexglobal%5Fcom%2FDocuments%2FPictures%2FScreenshots

Slab Area Calculation Formula:
Slab Area = (Length × Breadth) − (Less Length × Less Breadth)

Units of Measurement:
Measurements are taken in either centimetres (CM) or inches (INCH).

Conversion to Square Feet (SQFT):
To convert square centimetres (sqcm) to square feet (sqft):
Multiply by 0.00108

To convert square inches (sq inch) to square feet (sqft):
Divide by 144

Note: In India, Indian stones are measured at right angles.

faqs.question: How Indian stones are measured?
faqs.answer:
Measurement Method for Indian Stones

In India, Indian stones are measured at right angles. The standard method for calculating the slab area is:

Slab Area = (Centre of Length × Centre of Breadth) − (Broken Corners)

This approach ensures accurate measurement by considering the full rectangular area and deducting any broken or missing corners.

faqs.question: If a client has purchased 18mm flooring and needs more for same house , will 20mm now not hamper installation on same floor?
faqs.answer:
This will not be a problem, as it can be easily adjusted at the chemical level. Our processes are designed to accommodate such variations smoothly, ensuring consistent quality and performance.

Chemical-Level Adjustments: A Technical Perspective

There is no cause for concern, as the issue can be effectively resolved through chemical-level adjustments. In stone processing and surface treatment, minor inconsistencies—whether in texture, color tone, or surface finish—can be corrected using specialized chemical formulations and controlled application techniques.

How Chemical Adjustments Work:
Surface Treatment Agents:
These include resins, sealants, and polishing compounds that penetrate the stone surface to enhance gloss, fill micro-pores, and stabilize the structure. If there's a variation in sheen or finish, these agents can be tailored to balance the appearance.

pH and Reactivity Control:
Chemical treatments are designed to work within specific pH ranges to ensure compatibility with the stone's mineral composition. Adjustments in formulation allow for fine-tuning the reaction to achieve the desired surface characteristics without compromising durability.

Color Enhancement and Correction:
In cases where there are slight tonal differences, color enhancers or neutralizers can be applied to bring uniformity. These are especially useful in marble and granite processing where natural variation is common.

Durability Boosters:
Certain chemical additives are used to increase surface hardness and resistance to wear. These are particularly effective when working with thinner slabs or when upgrading from 18 MM to 20 MM, ensuring the thicker slab maintains its structural integrity and aesthetic appeal.

Controlled Application Techniques:
The success of chemical adjustments depends on precision in application—including temperature, curing time, and layering. Our trained technicians follow standard operating procedures to ensure consistent results across all slabs.

At Stonex, we leverage advanced chemical processing methods to ensure every slab meets our high standards of quality, sheen, and longevity. Even minor issues are addressed with scientific precision, ensuring that the final product is flawless and ready for luxury applications.

faqs.question: Explain polished vs epoxy slab color variation?
faqs.answer:
Understanding the Visual Difference: Polished vs. Epoxy-Coated Marble

Reference Image: https://stonexindia-my.sharepoint.com/my?id=%2Fpersonal%2Fprakhar%5Fgupta%5Fstonexglobal%5Fcom%2FDocuments%2FScreenshot%202026%2D02%2D19%20113958%2Epng&parent=%2Fpersonal%2Fprakhar%5Fgupta%5Fstonexglobal%5Fcom%2FDocuments 

The appearance of marble can vary significantly depending on the surface treatment. It’s similar to how your home floor looks lighter when dry and darker when wet—a subtle but noticeable shift in shade.

A polished surface reflects light more effectively, revealing brighter and more vibrant color tones.
An epoxy-coated surface, on the other hand, tends to absorb more light, resulting in a slightly darker appearance.
This difference is due to how light interacts with the surface:

Polished marble has a smooth, reflective finish that enhances the stone’s natural brightness.
Epoxy-coated marble creates a sealed layer that gives a wet-look effect, making the colors appear one notch deeper or darker.
So, when viewing an epoxy-coated slab, expect it to look visibly darker than its polished counterpart—even though the base material is the same.

faqs.question: Why do we recommend minimum 20-mm thick marble for flooring?
faqs.answer:
20 MM Marble: A Globally Recognized Standard
Globally, 20 MM thickness is considered essential to preserve the aesthetic value and structural integrity of marble over years of wear and tear. This standard is widely adopted across developed economies, where marble is used for cut-to-size custom tiles in high-end interior applications.

International & National Standards Supporting 20 MM
ASTM C1528-02: Defined by the American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM), a 125-year-old international organization, this standard outlines the recommended specifications for marble slabs used in architectural applications. It recognizes 20 MM thickness as the benchmark for durability and performance.

BIS IS 1130:1969: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) also defines marble slab specifications under IS 1130:1969, which includes thickness guidelines for blocks, slabs, and tiles used in construction and interior design.

These standards reinforce that 20 MM is not just a preference—it’s a performance-driven requirement for premium applications.

Why It Matters
Enhanced Flexural Strength: A 2 MM increase in thickness can improve the flexural strength of marble by up to 30%, making it more resistant to cracking and surface stress.
Long-Term Aesthetic Preservation: Thicker slabs retain their polish and finish longer, ensuring the marble continues to look luxurious for years.
Global Alignment: Using 20 MM aligns Indian offerings with international expectations, especially as Indian consumers increasingly seek global standards in home design.
At Stonex, we don’t just follow standards—we lead with them. By offering 20 MM marble, we ensure our clients receive world-class quality that stands the test of time.

faqs.question: Why do you recommend having 25 mm or more for stone in dry cladding?
faqs.answer:
Why Thickness Is Critical for Dry Cladding Applications
Dry cladding is a method of stone installation where the stone is mechanically fixed to a metal frame or structure—without the use of adhesives or cement. This technique is widely used in modern architecture for facades and exterior walls due to its durability and clean finish.

To securely install stone using dry cladding, the slab must undergo drilling of holes or cutting of slits to accommodate clamps, anchors, or brackets. These mechanical fixtures hold the stone in place.

Why Thickness Matters:
Structural Integrity: Thinner slabs are more prone to cracking or breaking during drilling or under load. A minimum thickness of 20 MM is essential to ensure the stone retains its strength and stability after modification.
Load Bearing: The stone must withstand wind pressure, thermal expansion, and mechanical stress over time. Thicker slabs offer better resistance to flexural and shear forces.
Safety & Longevity: In dry cladding, the stone is exposed to external elements. Thicker slabs provide greater durability, reducing the risk of failure and ensuring long-term performance.
At Stonex, we recommend and supply 20 MM marble slabs for dry cladding applications to meet both engineering requirements and aesthetic expectations.

faqs.question: Explain the application of our Italian marble mesh.
faqs.answer:
To ensure safe movement, lifting, and transportation of marble slabs, Italian fiber mesh is applied as a reinforcement layer. This mesh acts as a protective shield, reducing the risk of damage during handling.

Before installation, the mesh typically needs to be removed—either using a scraping tool or through a grinding process. In most cases, standard fiber mesh is easy to remove because its adhesion is not integral to the stone.

However, at Stonex, our Italian refinforced fiber mesh is engineered to become an integral part of the slab itself. This advanced bonding process significantly enhances the structural strength of the stone, making it more durable and resilient throughout its lifecycle.

The result?
A slab that’s not only protected during handling but also reinforced for long-term performance—without compromising on aesthetics.

faqs.question: Describe the white Italian marble porosity-related issues, solutions, and maintenance.
faqs.answer:
Porosity: is important to consider as it affects the product's wear and tear, plus its stain and germ absorption. According to Engineering Geology, water penetration can affect the stone's durability:

Issues:“Porosity plays a decisive role in decay processes of stones, as the pore network allows the water penetration responsible for the several physical and chemical deterioration processes affecting stone materials.”

With a higher volume of open spaces, it’s more likely bacteria and mould can enter these pores if left unsealed. 

Solutions: Higher porosity stones are sealed regularly to preserve their appearance, improve durability and increase resistance to stains and liquid by extending the time before the contaminant can be absorbed. Nonetheless, it’s valuable to consider the maintenance required along with a high-porosity stone.  

Maintenance: Regularly vacuum or dust mop Italian marble flooring to control dirt and residue buildup on it.
Clean up liquid spills instantly using a soft fabric or sponge to prevent staining.
Stop using powerful chemicals or acidic cleaners that can damage the marble's surface.
Occasionally polish the marble with a marble polish to preserve its natural shine

faqs.question: What are the advantages of utilizing natural stone?
faqs.answer:
Utilizing natural stone offers a plethora of advantages that contribute to its enduring popularity :
 
 Timeless Beauty: Natural stone boasts unique patterns, colors, and textures formed over millions of years, adding a timeless and distinctive aesthetic appeal to any space.
 
 Durability: Natural stone is exceptionally durable and can withstand heavy foot traffic, impact, and weathering, making it suitable for both indoor and outdoor applications.
 
 Versatility: With a wide range of types, including granite, marble, limestone, slate, and travertine, natural stone offers versatility in design, catering to diverse aesthetic preferences and functional needs.
 
 Longevity: Natural stone is renowned for its longevity, capable of retaining its beauty and structural integrity for generations with minimal maintenance.
 
 Sustainability: As a natural resource, stone is inherently sustainable when responsibly sourced and extracted. Additionally, its durability reduces the need for frequent replacements, minimizing environmental impact.
 
 Heat Resistance: Natural stone exhibits excellent heat resistance, making it an ideal choice for kitchen countertops, fireplace surrounds, and outdoor cooking areas.
 
 Variety of Finishes: Natural stone can be finished in various ways, including polished, honed, brushed, and textured, offering flexibility in achieving different visual effects and surface characteristics.
 
 Value Addition: Incorporating natural stone into architectural and interior design projects enhances property value due to its perceived luxury, durability, and timeless appeal.
 
 Low Maintenance: While periodic sealing and cleaning may be necessary, natural stone generally requires minimal maintenance compared to alternative materials, offering convenience and cost-effectiveness over time.
 
 Health Benefits: Natural stone is non-porous and does not harbor bacteria or allergens, promoting better indoor air quality and contributing to a healthier living environment.

faqs.question: In what areas of the home are natural stones suitable for use?
faqs.answer:
Natural stone offers unmatched versatility, making it suitable for a wide range of applications—without restrictions. Each type of stone brings its own strengths, making it ideal for specific uses:

Limestone, Granite, and Travertine: Perfect for facades and exterior applications due to their durability and weather resistance.
Marble: A timeless choice for interior spaces, offering elegance and refinement.
These stones vary in geological origin and chemical composition, which influence their texture, strength, and appearance. That’s why selecting the right stone involves both design preferences and functional requirements.

🛠️ Installation & Care
Different stones require distinct installation techniques and precautionary measures to ensure longevity and performance. Our skilled team is here to guide you through every step—helping you choose the right stone, the right finish, and the right method for your space.

Whether you're designing a home, a commercial space, or a luxury project, Stonex ensures expert advice and tailored solutions to bring your vision to life.

It’s important to note that comparing marble to tiles or vinyl is like comparing a handcrafted diamond to costume jewelry. While tiles and vinyl are manufactured in bulk, natural stone is quarried, selected, and processed with precision—and its value lies in its authenticity, longevity, and timeless appeal.

More luxurious and rare stones will never be inexpensive, because true luxury is defined by scarcity and quality.

The effectiveness of a surface sealant depends not only on the type of stone but also on how and where it’s used. To maintain the stone’s beauty and durability, resealing is essential at regular intervals.

🏢 Commercial Spaces
High foot traffic and frequent cleaning make these areas more prone to wear.
Recommended resealing: Every 2 years
🏠 Private Homes & Offices
Lower traffic and controlled environments reduce wear.
Recommended resealing: Every 5 years (based on general observations)
Our team at Stonex will guide you in selecting the right sealant based on:

Stone type
Surface finish
Area of application (wet vs. dry zones)
Expected usage and maintenance routines
Proper sealing not only protects your investment but also enhances the stone’s natural beauty for years to come.

faqs.question: Types of Sealant?

Hydrophobic,Oleophobic & Color enhancer are also a few types of sealers based on the protection they offer

faqs.question: Can natural stone experience cracking?
faqs.answer:
Natural stone cracking is a prevalent issue in India, often caused by:

Thermal Expansion & Contraction: Stones naturally expand and contract with temperature changes. However, the use of bud joints restricts this movement, leading to stress and eventual cracking.
Improper Thickness: Stones laid without adequate thickness are more prone to structural failure.
Installation Errors: Uneven substrates, air pockets, and water retention beneath the stone surface contribute significantly to post-installation cracks.
High TDS Water: Using water with high Total Dissolved Solids during installation can degrade stone integrity over time.
Preventive Measures
To minimize cracking, it's essential to:

Ensure a level and stable substrate.
Use correct installation techniques to avoid air pockets and water accumulation.
Apply suitable chemicals and sealants to protect the stone surface.
The SRS Advantage
Our 8-step SRS processed stones are engineered to address these challenges. This specialized treatment:

Enhances stone flexibility and durability.
Improves resistance to environmental stressors.
Ensures long-term aesthetic and structural integrity.

faqs.question: Can Natural Stone be used for a swimming pool and public areas? If so, what thickness is recommended?
faqs.answer:
Key Requirements for Pool-Safe Stone Installation
🧱 1. Low Absorption & Non-Slip Surface
Use stones with minimal water absorption to prevent damage and discoloration.
Ensure a non-slippery texture for safety in wet zones.
📏 2. Recommended Thickness
Minimum thickness: 20mm
Thickness should be proportional to the stone size to maintain structural integrity.
💧 3. Slope Management
Proper slope ensures efficient water drainage.
Smaller format stones are ideal for wet areas to facilitate slope creation and reduce cracking risks.
🧪 4. Adhesives & Grouting
Use high-quality adhesives suitable for wet environments.
Apply grouting to accommodate thermal expansion and contraction, preventing cracks and dislodgement..

faqs.question: Are there distinctions between Corian countertops and those made from Natural Stone?
faqs.answer:
Corian vs. Natural Stone Countertops
🧱 Natural Stone
Origin: Quarried from the earth or composed of natural aggregates.
Durability: Highly resistant to scratches, heat, and wear.
Aesthetic: Offers unique, natural patterns and color variations.
Customization: Available in various thicknesses starting from 20mm onwards.
Longevity: Ages beautifully and retains value over time.
🧪 Corian (Synthetic Solid Surface)
Origin: Man-made material composed of acrylic polymer and alumina trihydrate.
Cost: Slightly more cost-effective upfront.
Durability: More prone to scratching and heat damage.
Appearance: Uniform look, but lacks the natural variation of stone.
Maintenance: Easier to repair but may require more frequent care.
💡 Recommendation
While Corian may be suitable for budget-conscious projects, Natural Stone is the preferred choice for those seeking durability, natural beauty, and long-term value—especially in high-use areas like kitchens and poolside surfaces.

faqs.question: Does the price of marble vary depending on its color?
faqs.answer:
💰 Factors Influencing Marble Pricing
The cost of marble is influenced by several key factors:

🟢 1. Market Availability
Abundant availability of a particular color or type often results in lower pricing.
Rare or limited varieties tend to be more expensive due to demand and exclusivity.
⛰️ 2. Extraction Challenges
Difficulty in quarrying marble from mountainous or deep-earth locations can significantly increase costs.
Complex geological conditions may require specialized equipment and labor.
🚚 3. Transportation Expenses
Distance from quarry to site, mode of transport, and handling requirements all contribute to final pricing.
Heavier and thicker slabs incur higher shipping costs.

faqs.question: What factors contribute to the quality of Natural Stone?
faqs.answer: The grading of slabs is determined by various criteria impacting their quality and subsequently their cost. Several factors significantly affect quality. For example, the precision of slab cutting and polishing. Additionally, the presence of cracks or pits in the stone is a crucial technical consideration that our proprietary SRS Process addresses. Given its natural origin, individuals perceive color and character differently based on personal taste. What might appeal to one person may not necessarily appeal to another, but this doesn't denote poor quality in the stone. Natural variations in color and character are standard.When purchasing Natural Stone, it's important to ensure that the variations in stones are not excessively disparate and that there are no cracks. Multiple factors contribute to determining stone quality, such as the processing technology employed, color, consistency, rarity, thickness, and chemical composition. Even the same material from different sections of the same quarry may exhibit varying quality based on these characteristics.

faqs.question: Is it crucial for me to inspect the real slab rather than relying on a sample?
faqs.answer:
🎨 Natural Variation in Stone Appearance
We consider visual consistency highly significant when selecting natural stone. Due to its organic origin, each shipment may exhibit variations in tone, texture, and veining.

🌄 Why Variations Occur
Sediment differences from blocks extracted at varying depths within a mountain can lead to subtle color shifts.
Veins and patterns often span across the entire slab, which may not be visible in small samples.
📌 Important Note
A sample is only indicative of the stone’s color and character.
Being a natural material, we strongly recommend reviewing the full slab before making a final decision to ensure it aligns with your design expectations.

faqs.question: What are the different precautions we must keep while application of the stones?
faqs.answer:
Precautions for Installing Natural Stone
To ensure the longevity, safety, and aesthetic appeal of natural stone in various applications, follow these key precautions:

1. Surface Preparation
Clean, dry, and debris-free surfaces are crucial.
Proper preparation ensures strong adhesion and optimal performance.

2. Adhesive & Grout Selection
Use adhesives and grouts suited to the stone type and environment (interior/exterior, wet/dry).
Follow manufacturer instructions for mixing and application.

3. Handling & Transport
Handle with care to avoid breakage or scratches.
Use appropriate lifting tools and techniques for heavy pieces.

4. Acclimatization
Let stone materials adjust to the installation environment.
Prevents dimensional changes due to temperature and humidity shifts.

5. Sealing
Seal surfaces to protect against stains and moisture.
Apply sealants as per manufacturer guidelines.

6. Expansion Joints
Include joints to accommodate natural movement.
Prevents stress buildup and cracking.

7. Waterproofing
Essential in moisture-prone areas like kitchens, bathrooms, and outdoors.
Prevents water damage and mold growth.

8. Grout Cleanup
Clean excess grout promptly to avoid staining.
Use gentle cleaning methods and avoid harsh chemicals.

9. Protection During Construction
Shield installed stone from construction damage.
Use protective coverings like cardboard or film.

10. Regular Maintenance
Educate clients on cleaning, sealing, and inspections.
Ensures long-term beauty and performance.

faqs.question: What are the adhesives we should use for the Granite, marble, limestone, sand stones, slates, quartzite, basalt travertine, onyx & semiprecious ETC. stones?
faqs.answer:
S1 – Deformable Adhesive:
Offers flexibility with a deformability between 2.5 mm and 5 mm. Suitable for areas with moderate movement or thermal expansion.

S2 – Highly Deformable Adhesive:
Provides deformability above 5 mm, making it ideal for environments with significant structural movement, vibration, or thermal variation.

These classifications are especially important when selecting adhesives for natural stone or large-format tiles.

faqs.question: What are the different precautions we must take post-application of the above stones?
faqs.answer:
Natural Stone Maintenance Tips

1. Prevent Spills & Maintain Regular Cleaning
Prevention is key—avoid spills and stick to a consistent cleaning schedule.

2. Immediate Spill Response
Wipe up spills right away using a soft, gentle towel to prevent staining.

3. Use pH-Neutral Cleaners
Clean with pH-neutral solutions to preserve the stone’s integrity.

Do not use vinegar or acidic cleaners, as they can etch and damage the surface.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Cross cut and Vein cut and which stones can be cut in either or both?
faqs.answer:
The final decision on whether to use a cross or vein cut will always depend on preference. But if the intention is to book-match the slabs, then the right choice would be vein-cut slabs, which offer a parallel structure for a symmetrical look.

When a stone slab is vein-cut, it is cut against the natural grain of the stone, showcasing the banding and linear lines of the veining in the material.

When a stone slab is cross-cut, it is cut along the grain, revealing flowery, circular, and cloud-burst-like patterns.

faqs.question: What is SRS TUFF & why it is recommended and what is the process of SRS TUFF?
faqs.answer:
SRS TUFF – Advanced Densification for Softer Stones

SRS TUFF is a specialized densification process designed to enhance the stain resistance and hardness of softer stones such as limestones and white marbles. It uses an eco-friendly formulation that penetrates the stone from all sides, sealing pores with silica for long-lasting protection.

Process Overview:
Grinding
Slabs are ground to open the pores, preparing them for treatment.

Drying
Slabs are placed in ovens to remove moisture and ensure optimal absorption.

Dipping in SRS TUFF Tanks
Slabs are fully submerged for 36 to 48 hours in a controlled environment.
The eco-friendly solution impregnates the stone, sealing pores from all sides.

Curing & Polishing
After curing, slabs are polished to achieve the desired finish and shine.

Benefits:
Enhanced stain resistance
Improved hardness and durability
Ideal for softer stones prone to wear
Eco-friendly and sustainable process.

Disadvantages

If adhesive or cement is weak, stones may come loose.

Installation process is slower.

Advantages
Safe & durable:
Prevents chipping and collapse over time.

Weather adaptability:
Allows for expansion and contraction in extreme conditions.

Thermal insulation:
Air gap (30–45 mm) acts as a thermal barrier.

Aesthetic appeal:
Clean and visually pleasing finish.

Time-saving:
Faster installation process.

Disadvantages
Higher cost:
More expensive than wet cladding due to materials and installation complexity.
